title: Discovery Service API: Sentinel Onboarding Endpoint with mTLS and DID Proof-of-Possession
description: Implement the sentinel onboarding endpoint that validates the enrollment token, executes a DID-bound challenge-response proof-of-possession (PoP), and registers the sentinel identity. The endpoint performs a two-phase handshake: first a challenge issuance (if the client does not include a proof inline), then a proof submission that atomically consumes the token and creates the sentinel record. mTLS is enforced at the transport layer. The endpoint is idempotent for retries.
objectives: Cryptographically binding the sentinel DID to the enrollment token — no impersonation possible
Atomic one-time token consumption preventing replay or double-registration
Idempotent behavior for retried registrations (network failures during onboarding)

steps: Step 1 - Transport Security: Enforce mTLS for this endpoint. The client (sentinel) must present a valid client TLS certificate. At minimum in dev/test: mutual TLS with a self-signed CA managed by the environment. In prod: use the government PKI or a dedicated sentinel CA. The TLS client certificate CN/SAN does NOT grant authorization by itself — it is an additional channel binding, not the sole auth factor.

Step 2 - Two-Phase Handshake Design: Phase 1 (Challenge): POST /api/v1/sentinels/onboard with only { enrollment_token, did } in body. If no proof field is present, validate the token (signature, exp, hash lookup, status=APPROVED), generate a nonce, store it in Redis with key onboard:nonce:{jti} TTL=120s, return 200 { challenge_nonce, challenge_expires_at }. Phase 2 (Proof): POST /api/v1/sentinels/onboard with { enrollment_token, did, proof: { type, created, challenge_nonce, proof_value } }. Process full onboarding.

Step 3 - Enrollment Token Validation: Extract JWT from enrollment_token field. Verify JWT signature using Discovery issuer public key. Check: exp > now(), iss == DISCOVERY_DID. Compute SHA-256 of submitted token string; look up token_hash in enrollment_tokens where jti matches JWT.jti. Verify status=APPROVED. If any check fails → 401 or 403 with specific error code.

Step 4 - DID Proof-of-Possession Verification: Supported DID method for MVP: did:key (self-contained, no resolution needed). Resolve the public key from the did:key DID. Verify the proof_value (a JWS/signature) over canonical PoP payload: { jti, did, challenge_nonce, iat }. PoP payload must be serialized as compact JSON (sorted keys). Signature must use the same algorithm as registered in DID doc (Ed25519/P-256). Reject if iat is more than 30 seconds old (clock drift protection).

Step 5 - Nonce Validation: Retrieve nonce from Redis key onboard:nonce:{jti}. Verify that challenge_nonce in request matches. Delete the Redis key immediately after retrieval (one-time use). If key missing (expired or already used) → 401 NONCE_EXPIRED.

Step 6 - Atomic Token Consumption: Call EnrollmentTokenRepository.consume_atomic(session, jti). Uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to prevent concurrent consumption. Sets status='CONSUMED', consumed_at=now(). Returns the token record or raises TokenAlreadyConsumedError. On TokenAlreadyConsumedError → 409 ENROLLMENT_ALREADY_CONSUMED.

Step 7 - Sentinel Record Creation (Idempotent): Check if a sentinel with (did, role, env) already exists. If exists and status=active → treat as idempotent re-registration (return existing record + fresh config). If exists and status=decommissioned → re-activate with new registered_at. If not exists → INSERT into sentinels: { id (uuid4), service_id, did, role, env, is_active=true, created_at=now(), last_seen=now(), config_version=1 }.

Step 8 - Response Bundle: Return 201 (first registration) or 200 (re-registration) with: { sentinel_id, did, role, env, config_version, discovery: { onboard_url, sync_url, credentials_url }, chain: { network, rpc_urls, contract_addresses: { issuer_registry, trust_policy_registry, status_registry, service_registry } }, revocation: { delta_seconds: 300 }, observability: { otlp_endpoint } }. This bundle bootstraps the sentinel without any additional HTTP calls for basic operation.

Step 9 - Initial Config Bundle + Credentials: After sentinel record creation, call ConfigBundleService.generate_and_sign(sentinel_id) to produce version-1 config bundle (TASK-027). If TASK-028 is implemented: call CredentialIssuerService.issue_sentinel_identity(sentinel) to issue the initial SentinelIdentityCredential. Include credential JWT in response under credentials.sentinel_identity if ready, or indicate credentials_pending=true if issued asynchronously.

Step 10 - Audit Event: Write audit_event: action='sentinel_onboarded', actor_type='SENTINEL_ONBOARDING', actor_id=did, target_type='sentinel', target_id=sentinel_id, summary={ service_id, role, env, token_jti }. Also write: action='enrollment_token_consumed', actor_id=did, target_id=token_id.

Step 11 - Rate Limiting and Anti-Flood: Rate limit POST /api/v1/sentinels/onboard: 5 attempts per token_jti (rejects flood). Redis key: ratelimit:onboard:{jti}, TTL=600. Additionally: 50 total onboarding attempts per client IP per 10 minutes. Return 429 with proper error on exceed.

Step 12 - Idempotency Key Support: Client may send Idempotency-Key: <uuid4> header. Cache the onboarding response keyed by Idempotency-Key in Redis (TTL 10 minutes). Return cached response on duplicate. Prevents double-registration from network retries after server already committed.

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/service: DID resolution and PoP signature verification

structure.proof_value: base64url-encoded signature over canonical PoP payload

error_handling.message: Enrollment token has not been approved
Challenge nonce expired or already used. Request a new challenge.
This enrollment token has already been used
Only did:key and did:web are supported in this environment
Too many onboarding attempts. Retry after 60s

security_constraints: mTLS enforced at reverse proxy/load balancer level for this endpoint; requests without client cert rejected with 400
Challenge nonce stored in Redis with TTL=120s; deleted on first use — one-time, cannot be replayed
PoP payload signed by sentinel must include jti + did + challenge_nonce + iat. Any of these missing → reject
iat in PoP must be within 30 seconds of server time — reject stale proofs to prevent delayed replay
Enrollment token raw value never logged — only jti is logged in audit events
Token consumption is atomic (SELECT FOR UPDATE) — concurrent onboarding with same token → exactly one succeeds
Idempotency-Key caching prevents accidental double-registration from network retries

enrollment_tokens_repository.consume_atomic: consume_atomic(session: AsyncSession, jti: str) -> EnrollmentToken | raises TokenAlreadyConsumedError | raises TokenNotFoundError

did_verification_service.verify_pop: verify_pop(did: str, pop_payload: dict, proof_value: str) -> bool | raises DIDResolutionError | raises InvalidSignatureError

nonce_store.consume_nonce: consume_nonce(jti: str, provided_nonce: str) -> bool (False if not found or mismatch)

acceptance_criteria: Onboarding with invalid/expired/not-approved token returns 401/403 with correct error code
Onboarding with invalid DID PoP signature returns 401 INVALID_DID_PROOF
Two concurrent onboarding requests with the same token — exactly one returns 201, the other returns 409
Onboarding with a replayed challenge nonce returns 401 NONCE_EXPIRED
Idempotent re-submission with same Idempotency-Key returns cached 201 response without re-executing
sentinel_id and did appear in audit_events after successful onboarding
Returned bundle contains all chain contract addresses and revocation delta
